text: **Anna:** We are back with Anna and {Caroline|女性の名前} and the {bees|蜂|/biːz/|Bees make honey in their hive}.
**Anna:** Last time Anna was {afraid|恐れている|/əˈfreɪd/|I am afraid of spiders}.
**Anna:** I'm not afraid of {beef|牛肉|/biːf/|Beef is a type of meat}.
**Anna:** But Caroline is helping her to stay {safe|安全な|/seɪf/|Stay safe on the road}.
**Anna:** Look at {sentences|文|/ˈsentənsəz/|Write complete sentences.} that start with if to find more {conditional|条件の|/kənˈdɪʃənəl/|Conditional sentences use if.} sentences
**Anna:** Remember, type 1 {conditionals|条件文|/kənˈdɪʃənəlz/|Conditionals explain possible situations.} use the present tense and will.
**Anna:** And type 2 use the past tense and would.
**Anna:** Now let's find out what happens with Anna and the bees
**Anna:** I have a {feeling|感覚/感情|/ˈfiːlɪŋ/|I have a good feeling about this.} that your fear of bees will be gone soon.
**Anna:** There are things {beekeepers|養蜂家|/ˈbiːkiːpərz/|Beekeepers work with bees.} do to protect ourselves.
**Anna:** For example, we wear {protective|防護の|/prəˈtektɪv/|Protective gear keeps you safe.} clothing.
**Caroline:** This is a {beekeeper's|養蜂家の|/ˈbiːkiːpərz/|Beekeeper's tools are special.} veil, suit, and gloves.
**Anna:** There are other things we can do too.
**Anna:** If I use the {smoker|煙発生器|/ˈsmoʊkər/|Smokers calm bees down.}, the bees will stay calm.
**Anna:** Let's use smoke.
**Anna:** Lots of smoke.
**Caroline:** Okay Anna, that's enough smoke
**Caroline:** Another thing you can do is stay calm.
**Caroline:** Okay, when I lift the {frame|枠/フレーム|/freɪm/|A frame holds honeycomb.}, you will see the bees
**Anna:** I'm ready.
**Anna:** Let's do this thing.
**Caroline:** Okay, let's put on our {protective|防護の|/prəˈtektɪv/|Protective clothing prevents injury.} clothing.
**Caroline:** I will carefully take out a frame.
**Caroline:** This is where the bees live.
**Caroline:** There are {worker|労働者|/ˈwɜːrkər/|Workers do important jobs.} bees, {drone|雄蜂|/droʊn/|Drone bees do not work.} bees, and the queen bee.
**Caroline:** The bees have different jobs.
**Caroline:** The queen bee lays the eggs.
**Anna:** The worker bees collect {nectar|蜜|/ˈnektər/|Nectar is sweet liquid.} and {pollen|花粉|/ˈpɒlən/|Pollen helps plants reproduce.} from flowers and bring it back to the hive.
**Anna:** Then they make honey.
**Anna:** Now you hold the frame
**Caroline:** There are the {bees|蜂|/biːz/|Bees make honey}.
**Anna:** There are so many.
**Anna:** And they are so {busy|忙しい/活発な|/ˈbɪzi/|Busy workers help the team}.
**Anna:** Now I know why we say {busy|忙しい|/ˈbɪzi/|Busy people work hard} as a bee.
**Caroline:** Good job, Anna
**Anna:** Caroline, bees are {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|Beautiful flowers bloom} and {important|重要な|/ɪmˈpɔːrtənt/|Important decisions matter}.
**Anna:** If I wanted to help the bees, what would I do?
**Caroline:** Great question.
**Anna:** You can plant {native|固有の/土着の|/ˈneɪtɪv/|Native plants grow naturally} flowers that bees like.
**Anna:** Here's a book to help you know which ones to plant.
**Anna:** It's a beautiful book.
**Anna:** And look, it tells you which bees like which types of flowers.
**Caroline:** It also tells you how the flowers affect the {flavor|風味/味|/ˈfleɪvər/|Flavor makes food delicious} of the honey.
**Anna:** Flowers make a big {difference|違い/影響|/ˈdɪfərəns/|Small differences matter} to bees.
**Anna:** If they don't eat, they won't live.
**Anna:** Buying {local|地元の|/ˈloʊkəl/|Local shops support community} honey also helps the bees.
**Anna:** If you want to help bees, do those two things.
**Professor Bot:** I learned a lot about bees.
**Anna:** Hey, if I worked with bees, I would not need {protective|防護の|/prəˈtektɪv/|Protective gear keeps you safe} clothing.
**Anna:** Guess what?
**Anna:** That was a type 2 {conditional|条件の|/kənˈdɪʃənəl/|Conditional sentences use 'if'}.
**Anna:** And I heard a type 1 {conditional|条件の|/kənˈdɪʃənəl/|Conditional sentences use 'if'}.
**Anna:** If they don't eat, they won't live.
**Anna:** Thanks for the book Caroline and the bee {puppet|人形/操り人形|/ˈpʌpət/|Puppet shows are fun} and for teaching me about bees.
**Anna:** Look
**Anna:** I'm not scared.
**Caroline:** Good job, Anna.
**Anna:** If you ever want to see the bees again, I'll be here.
**Anna:** You have my {business|事業/商売|/ˈbɪznəs/|Business cards share contact} card.
**Anna:** I'll let you see yourself out.
**Anna:** Thanks, bye.
**Anna:** I will {plant|植える/育てる|/plænt/|I plant flowers in my garden.} tons of {flowers|花|/ˈflaʊərz/|Flowers bloom in spring.}
**Anna:** Thanks, Arna.
**Anna:** Sure thing, bees.
**Anna:** Oh, and I'm sorry about {hitting|打つ/当たる|/ˈhɪtɪŋ/|I'm hitting the ball.} that {hive|巣/蜂の巣|/haɪv/|Bees live in a hive.} when I was a little girl.
**Bees:** We love you, Anna.
**Anna:** Take care, bees
**Anna:** Which way is the {metro|地下鉄|/ˈmetrəʊ/|The metro is fast.}?
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** So if you want to learn more about {bees|蜂|/biːz/|Bees make honey.}, visit your local {beekeeper|養蜂家|/ˈbiːkiːpər/|A beekeeper takes care of bees.}, like Anna did.
**Anna:** Maybe you can try some local {honey|蜂蜜|/ˈhʌni/|Honey is sweet.} too.
**Anna:** Now it's your turn.
**Anna:** Practice making {conditional|条件の|/kənˈdɪʃənl/|Conditional sentences use 'if'.} sentences.
**Anna:** If you practice, you will become better at them.
**Anna:** That's type 1.
